title: Selective mutism - NHS
text:
ur child. Ask friends and relatives to give your child time to warm up at his or her own pace and focus on fun activities rather than getting them to talk. As well as verbal reassurance, give them love, support and patience. 
Getting help and support 
The  Royal College of Speech and Language Therapists and the Association of Speech and Language Therapists in Independent Practice  can help you find professionals in your area with experience in treating selective mutism.
